File row linked to the chosen project/profile, so the existing OCR +
correction + training pipeline handles each page unchanged.

profile), explanatory copy on both sections, empty-state prompts when
the user has no projects or profiles yet, visible upload progress
("Splitting PDF…"), and error alerts when an upload fails.

cd "/Users/shmuelsokol/Desktop/CURSOR AI/ocr-hebrew/web" && git add CLAUDE.md Dockerfile package.json src/app/api/health/route.ts wiki/ scripts/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Harden DB safety after production schema wipe

The shared Supabase project `ushngszdltlctmqlwgot` silently lost all
OCR Hebrew tables from the public schema; every API request was
returning P2021 "table does not exist" errors. This commit adds guards
so the same class of incident is detected immediately and cannot
recover-over data silently on boot.

- Dockerfile: remove `prisma db push` from CMD. Schema changes are now
  a deliberate, separate step — the container no longer mutates schema
  on every deploy.
- /api/health: verify the existence of User, Project, HandwritingProfile,
  File, TrainingExample. Returns 503 schema-missing if any are gone so
  Railway health checks fail loudly instead of serving a broken app.
- npm run db:push:prod: guarded wrapper around prisma db push. Refuses
  when the DB already holds rows unless CONFIRM_SCHEMA_CHANGE=yes is set.
- npm run db:backup: dumps every user-table to timestamped JSON with
  30-day rotation. Intended to run nightly via cron on the Mac Mini.
- scripts/import-training-data.js: recovery tool that rebuilds
  TrainingExample rows from the local training/data/labels.json backup.
- wiki/safety.md + decisions log + CLAUDE.md: document what happened,
  the rules going forward, and the recovery runbook.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push
cd "/Users/shmuelsokol/Desktop/CURSOR AI/ocr-hebrew/web" && set -a && source .env.local && set +a && npm run db:push:prod 2>&1 | tail -30
mkdir -p "/Users/shmuelsokol/Desktop/CURSOR AI/ocr-hebrew/web/wiki/migration"

cd "/Users/shmuelsokol/Desktop/CURSOR AI/ocr-hebrew/web" && git add wiki/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add migration plan + briefings for 3rdBHMK and 3D Images

OCR Hebrew, 3rdBHMK, and 3D Images all share one Supabase project.
Prisma's db push treats tables not in its schema as drop candidates,
so any push from one app endangers the others' data — this is the
root cause of the 2026-04-19 OCR Hebrew wipe.

Plan is to give 3rdBHMK and 3D Images each their own dedicated
Supabase project, leaving ushngszdltlctmqlwgot exclusive to OCR Hebrew
(which already has its Storage here — moving would risk the 2,450
training + page images).

Each brief is a self-contained runbook the other apps' Claude sessions
can execute:
- wiki/migration/README.md — plan overview + hand-off message
- wiki/migration/3rdbhmk-brief.md — dump bhmk_* tables + bhmk schema,
  restore to new project, migrate bhmk Storage bucket, update Railway
  env, verify, add safety guards, drop from shared project
- wiki/migration/3d-images-brief.md — same for td_* tables and
  3d-images Storage bucket
- wiki/migration/ocr-hebrew-recovery-brief.md — the OCR Hebrew recovery
  steps to run once both other apps report complete

OCR Hebrew recovery (schema push, training re-import, user re-register,
nightly backup cron) is blocked on the two other apps reporting done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push

Add Settings page, top nav, typed-confirm delete; fix import script

Destructive actions (delete project / handwriting profile) are gone
from the dashboard. They now live on a new /settings page behind a
typed-confirmation modal — the user must type the exact item name
before the delete button enables. The modal spells out exactly what
data is lost vs preserved.

TopNav component gives consistent navigation across pages
(Dashboard, Training, Dictionary, Settings, Sign out), starting with
the dashboard and settings routes.

Dashboard keeps the ability to view/select profiles and open projects;
the X delete buttons are replaced with a discreet "manage in Settings"
link so projects and profiles can still be curated — just not
discarded by an accidental click.

import-training-data.js: fix relative path to labels.json (was off
by one ..) and rewrite to upload from training/data/images/ into
Storage at training/<userId>/<profileId>/<id>.jpg, then insert
TrainingExample rows pointing at the new paths. Used today to restore
2,185 examples after the 2026-04-19 wipe — 0 failures.
